title: Fetch, Response Admission, Abort Signals, Time Budgets, and Error Causes · Contract Lens: Pokedex Ts Response Byte Boundary
summary: The client calls fetch globally, assumes only rejection means failure, parses every status as Pokemon, creates timeout controllers that outlive requests, and loses abort causes. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts response byte boundary increment in injected bounded abort-aware Fetch client. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Bound response bytes before or while consuming, reject truncated or oversized data, and avoid unbounded diagnostic copies.
Compose caller cancellation with bounded timeout using AbortSignal APIs and preserve the first meaningful reason.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
